title: ISCI — Claude as an Auditable Scientific Judge
thesis: Magnitude says how far a cell moved; controllability asks where it moved, whether it repeated, and whether the evidence deserves a PASS.

verdict_contract.PASS: Prespecified direction and required gates are met.
verdict_contract.FAIL: The claim is evaluable but a directional or required gate is violated.
verdict_contract.NULL: The claim is evaluable but no supported incremental signal is present.
verdict_contract.NOT-EVALUABLE: A required input or structural gate is absent; no biological conclusion is forced.

claims.claim: Among detectable-effect perturbations, axis-specificity and cross-donor coherence identify canonical T-cell state regulators beyond effect magnitude.
claims.scope: Detectable-effect perturbations and canonical axis-defining T-cell regulators.
claims.prohibited_overclaim: Broad functional-regulator discovery or therapeutic target nomination.

claims.claim: Magnitude-conditional controllership generalizes to an independent set of non-marker functional regulators.
claims.scope: Twenty external non-marker regulators with matched negatives.
claims.prohibited_overclaim: The failed broad class invalidates the bounded canonical-regulator result.

claims.claim: A perturbational IEC axis transports as a multi-study CAR-T response predictor.
claims.scope: Eighty-seven labeled patients across nine public studies.
claims.prohibited_overclaim: Proof of no biological effect or a validated clinical biomarker.

claims.claim: scGPT embeddings independently separate controllers from matched negatives.
claims.scope: Current machine state and the pre-specified expression-profile input contract.
claims.prohibited_overclaim: A scientific null, failed foundation model, or negative biological result.
